data.title: T2 Metals boosts private financing to raise $7.6M at $0.50 per unit
data.description:
• T2 Metals (TMC) increased its non-brokered private placement financing by up to $2.6M due to strong demand.
  • The company proposed to issue up to 15.2M units at $0.50 each to raise up to $7.6M.
  • Each unit included one common share and one-half a warrant. Each full warrant entitled holders to purchase one additional share at $0.75 within two years of closing.
  • Finder’ fees may be paid on part of the financing, with net proceeds used for exploration and working capital.
  • All issued securities had a four-month hold period and required approval from the TSX Venture Exchange.
  • CEO Mark Saxon said strong interest from new and existing investors has led the Company to increase the financing, adding that its Yukon gold and silver assets are attracting attention ahead of a successful 2026.

data.title: Amazon to charge for 'Prime Video Ultra' separately in the U.S.
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) said Friday its ad-free Prime Video subscription plan will be rebranded as "Prime Video Ultra" from April 10, and users will be charged separately on top of their existing membership.

Prime Video Ultra will be priced at $4.99 a month or at $45.99 for the year and is currently only for U.S. members.

Amazon said the price for standard Prime membership for online shopping, food ordering, and ad-supported content streaming is unchanged at $14.99 a month or $139 a year.

"Delivering ad-free streaming with premium features requires significant investment, and this structure aligns with other major streaming services while ensuring customers have the flexibility to choose how they want to watch," the company said in its blog.

The new plan allows up to five concurrent streams, bumped up from three; up to 100 downloads, raised from 25; and exclusive access to 4K/UHD streaming, among other things.

data.title: ONE Group targets $840M–$855M revenue in 2026 as portfolio conversions accelerate
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: The ONE Group Hospitality, Inc. (STKS) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Emanuel Hilario, President, CEO & Director, highlighted that "total GAAP revenue for the full year 2025 was approximately $805 million, representing approximately 20% growth year-over-year, driven primarily by the inclusion of Benihana for all 12 periods." He stated, "full year 2025 comparable sales declined approximately 3.7%, reflecting continued pressure across the full service dining segment," and explained that Q4 total GAAP revenue was approximately $207 million compared to $222 million in the prior year quarter, mainly due to portfolio optimization actions and a fiscal calendar shift.
  • Hilario emphasized sequential improvement in consolidated comparable sales, noting, "all brands demonstrated sequential improvement in comparable sales during the quarter. That momentum has accelerated to 2026. That was not just a holiday spike. This is sustained execution." He added that both Benihana and STK are positive in sales year-to-date, and Kona Grill's turnaround is "gaining traction."
  • Strategic priorities for 2026 include accelerating same-store sales through execution, capital-efficient growth with disciplined expansion, portfolio optimization to improve returns, and maintaining balance sheet strength and flexibility. Hilario detailed, "we are guiding to a 1% to 3% increase [in same-store sales] this year," and noted progress with operational efficiencies, menu innovation, and loyalty programs.
  • The company entered into two significant asset-light development agreements in Q4, securing development rights for 10 Benihana and Benihana Express locations in California and commitments for additional franchise and licensed locations in Florida. Hilario described these as "the largest asset-light development agreement in the company's history."
  • Nicole Thaung, Chief Financial Officer, stated, "total consolidated GAAP revenues were $207 million, decreasing 6.7% from $222 million for the same quarter last year." She added, "company-owned restaurant cost of sales as a percentage of company-owned restaurant net revenue improved 80 basis points to 19.6% from 20.4%."

Outlook

  • The company projects total GAAP revenues between $840 million and $855 million for fiscal year 2026, with anticipated consolidated comparable sales growth of 1% to 3%.
  • Management franchise and license revenues are expected to be between $14 million and $15 million, adjusted EBITDA between $100 million and $110 million, and general and administration costs, excluding stock-based compensation, at approximately $53 million.
  • The company plans to open 6 to 10 new venues in 2026. Guidance for company-owned operating expenses as a percentage of company-owned restaurant net revenue is approximately 82% to 83%.
  • Hilario indicated that cost predictability has been achieved through strategic protein sourcing and pricing contracts, which are expected to contribute to margin improvements through September 2026.

Financial Results

  • For Q4, total consolidated GAAP revenues were $207 million, and company-owned restaurants net revenue was $203 million. Management, licensed, franchise, and incentive fee revenues were $4 million.
  • Restaurant operating profit, excluding closed or to-be-closed Grill Concepts restaurants, was $38.9 million or 19.5% of owned restaurant net revenue.
  • Net loss attributable to The ONE Group Hospitality, Inc. was $6.4 million, compared to net income of $1.6 million in Q4 2024. Net loss available to common stockholders was $15.3 million or ($0.49) net loss per share.
  • Adjusted EBITDA attributable to The ONE Group Hospitality was $28.1 million compared to $31 million in the prior year quarter.
  • The company recorded noncash impairment charges of $7.2 million, primarily related to the Grill portfolio optimization, and ended the quarter with $4.7 million in cash and cash equivalents.

Q&A

  • Allison Arfstrom, Piper Sandler, asked about strategic priorities for Benihana. Hilario responded, "our priority for Benihana right now continues to be marketing, working on digital, working on friends with benefits... continue to working on bringing a smaller sized menu... operations and turn times and improving... turn times at the table and overall guest experience."
  • Joseph Gomes, NOBLE Capital, questioned the gap between Q4 revenue guidance and actual results. Hilario explained, "we thought we could get more out of the Benihana brand, particularly with table turns... we weren't able to achieve the full... synergies that we wanted to do on table turns."
  • Gomes also inquired about remaining Benihana acquisition synergies. Hilario outlined ongoing benefits from procurement consolidation, particularly beef and other cost categories, stating, "I still think that there's some benefits... we will benefit for in 2026."
  • Anthony Lebiedzinski, Sidoti, asked about regional traffic differences and pricing. Hilario noted, "the geographical differences that we saw in the third quarter narrowed down in the fourth."
  • Mark Smith, Lake Street Capital Markets, inquired about conversion timelines. Hilario projected that five closed restaurants in conversion mode would reopen by July 2026.
  • James Sanderson, Northcoast Research, asked about driving targeted traffic and product innovation. Hilario highlighted early-stage progress in takeout, loyalty, and event business, aiming for takeout delivery to reach 20% of sales.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts repeatedly pressed for clarity on revenue miss explanations, synergy realization, and regional performance, reflecting a slightly negative tone, particularly around execution risk and guidance credibility.
  • Management's sentiment was confident and operationally focused in prepared remarks, but slightly defensive during Q&A, as evidenced by Hilario stating, "we weren't able to achieve the full... synergies that we wanted to do on table turns."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone showed improvement in operational momentum but ongoing scrutiny from analysts regarding execution and traffic drivers.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management guidance shifted from projecting negative comparable sales in 2025 to forecasting 1% to 3% growth in 2026. The strategic focus has evolved from broad portfolio optimization and cost containment to targeted growth via conversions, asset-light expansion, and enhanced operational rigor.
  • Analyst focus moved from concerns about pricing and regional headwinds to deeper questions on execution, cost synergies, and the timeline for conversion reopenings.
  • Net revenue declined quarter-over-quarter, with higher impairment charges in Q4 tied to ongoing portfolio optimization.
  • Sentiment from both management and analysts remained cautious, though improved operational results and forward guidance signaled rising confidence.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged ongoing consumer confidence challenges and cost inflation.
  • Portfolio optimization actions, including closures and impairment charges, continue to impact near-term revenues.
  • Uncertainty around gas prices and macroeconomic volatility was cited as a potential risk to traffic trends.
  • Analysts questioned the repeatability of recent conversion successes and the realization of synergy targets.

Final Takeaway

The company is positioning itself for a return to growth in 2026, emphasizing operational discipline, portfolio optimization, and capital-efficient expansion. Management projects a 1% to 3% increase in consolidated comparable sales and $840 million to $855 million in total GAAP revenues for the year, underpinned by ongoing brand innovation, asset-light development agreements, and cost management strategies. The focus remains on driving sustainable traffic, expanding the Benihana and STK brands, and maintaining financial flexibility amid evolving market conditions.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Crypto stocks gain as Bitcoin sell-offs could reportedly be entering the final phase
data.description:
Crypto stocks were gaining late Friday morning as Bitcoin and related cryptocurrency sell-offs could reportedly be entering their final phase.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is on track to close the week ~9% higher, shrugging off the Middle East concerns impacting broader markets. Ethereum (ETH-USD) is set to add ~11%.

The gains come alongside hopes of more crypto-friendly regulation in the U.S.

On March 11, the Securities and Exchange Commission and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission announced an MoU and coordination for "providing a fit-for-purpose regulatory framework for crypto assets and other emerging technologies", among other things.

Last week, cryptocurrency investment products recorded inflows of $619M.

The crypto sell-off could be entering its final phase, Bloomberg News reported, citing Brett Munster of Blockforce Capital.

Munster tracks four measures to gauge where Bitcoin stands in its crash cycle. BTC seems to have already grazed the upper edge of what he considers a probable bottoming zone, the Friday, March 13, report noted.

The majority of the drawdown appears to be behind us, and the asymmetry is shifting, he reportedly said, adding that a potential turnaround could come about mid-year.

"Once selling pressure fades, even modest new inflows can move the market," the crypto fund manager who has invested through three prior boom-and-bust cycles reportedly told the news outlet.

Crypto-linked stocks are trading higher on the back of the recent uptick in the prices of the digital assets. Notable moves as of Friday morning include:

  • MARA Holdings (MARA) - ~14% higher
  • Cipher Digital (CIFR) - ~10% higher
  • Bitmine Immersion (BMNR) - ~8% higher
  • CleanSpark (CLSK) - ~7.5% higher
  • Bakkt (BKKT) - ~6% higher
  • Strategy (MSTR) - ~5% higher
  • IREN (IREN) - ~4.5% higher
  • Riot Platforms (RIOT), Bullish (BLSH), Coinbase Global (COIN) - ~4% higher

data.title: Amazon collabs with Cerebras to deploy AI inference solutions in data centers
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) is collaborating with Cerebras (CBRS) to deploy a new AI data center solution designed to increase inference speed.

The partnership makes Amazon Web Services the first major cloud provider to offer the up-and-coming AI chipmaker's disaggregated inference solution. The companies will combine AWS Trainium-powered servers, Cerebras CS-3 systems, and Elastic Fabric Adapter networking. 

"Inference is where AI delivers real value to customers, but speed remains a critical bottleneck for demanding workloads like real-time coding assistance and interactive applications," said David Brown, Vice President, Compute & ML Services, AWS. "What we're building with Cerebras solves that: by splitting the inference workload across Trainium and CS-3, and connecting them with Amazon’s Elastic Fabric Adapter, each system does what it's best at. The result will be inference that's an order of magnitude faster and higher performance than what's available today."

It will be deployed on Amazon Bedrock, which allows developers to customize, secure, and deploy AI models without needing to manage infrastructure. AWS also plans to offer leading open-source large language models and Amazon Nova using Cerebras hardware later this year.

"Partnering with AWS to build a disaggregated inference solution will bring the fastest inference to a global customer base," said Andrew Feldman, founder and CEO of Cerebras Systems. "Every enterprise around the world will be able to benefit from blisteringly fast inference within their existing AWS environment."

Cerebras partners with some of the top developers of LLMs, such as Meta Platforms (META) and OpenAI (OPENAI). It also competes against AI chip titans, such as Nvidia (NVDA). Earlier this year, it completed a $1B Series H funding round, valuing the company at $23B.

data.title: PagerDuty downgraded by William Blair after 'disappointing' quarter, guidance
data.description:
PagerDuty (PD) was downgraded by William Blair on Friday after the software company reported what the investment firm called a “disappointing” quarter.

“PagerDuty reported largely disappointing fourth-quarter results, with ARR growth of only $2 million sequentially and 1% year-over-year (missing Street estimates by 60 basis points) and net retention that fell by 2 percentage points sequentially to 98%,” analyst Patrick McIlwee wrote in a note to clients. “Management attributed this weakness to ongoing seat-based headwinds, which it expects to persist in the near term. The company provided a disappointing outlook for growth in fiscal 2027, calling for flat revenue growth at the midpoint (prior consensus had growth of 4%), and flat operating margins relative to fiscal 2026.”

McIlwee lowered his rating on PagerDuty to Market Perform from Market Outperform. He pointed out that while the stock does not have a high valuation, he does not see “a clear near-term catalyst” any time soon.

“We acknowledge that PagerDuty is a best-in-class solution addressing a sizable market opportunity; however, we view its incident management solutions as more of a point-solution, and we believe the business is increasingly exhibiting pressures from both: 1) larger, more established platforms encroaching on its offering, and 2) seat-based compression among its customer base (whether or not from the impacts of AI),” McIlwee added. “We acknowledge that the company is still early in its transition to usage-based pricing, but we believe it has much work to do on this front before reestablishing a sustainable growth algorithm, and therefore see the risk/reward setup as balanced.”

PagerDuty shares fell 3% in late morning trading.

data.title: Fed rate cuts would backfire amid oil supply shock, Jim Bianco warns
data.description:
Macro analyst Jim Bianco cautioned Friday that Federal Reserve rate cuts would be counterproductive in the wake of an oil supply shock, arguing that such a move would send bond yields soaring and worsen inflationary pressures.

In a recent post on X, the head of Bianco Research outlined why the central bank should remain on the sidelines as crude oil prices surge due to supply constraints stemming from shipping difficulties through the Strait of Hormuz.

“If the Fed cut rates, bond yields would soar,” Bianco wrote. “The market needs demand destruction to restore balance.”

According to Bianco, the current spike in oil prices represents the market’s natural mechanism for rationing scarce supply. With tankers unable to move freely through the critical shipping chokepoint, there simply isn’t enough crude oil to meet demand, forcing the market to push prices higher until marginal buyers step back.

The analyst warned that Fed intervention would trigger a dangerous feedback loop. If policymakers attempt to offset rising energy costs by cutting rates, markets would respond by driving prices even higher—a cycle that would only end when enough consumers stop purchasing oil and gasoline.

“This vicious cycle would be viewed as inflation returning, like 2022, and the bond market would sell off hard, sending yields soaring,” Bianco explained.

His comments come a day after U.S. President Donald Trump took to his social media platform Truth Social to urge Powell to cuts rates now and not wait for the next Federal Open Market Committee rate decision on March 18. But traders are no longer fully pricing in a one-quarter reduction this year as higher oil prices fuel inflation fears.

Treasury ETFs: (TLT), (TLH), (IEF), (IEI), (SHY), (SGOV), (SCHO), and (BIL).

Oil ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (DBO), (OILK), and (USL).

Market-tracking funds: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: DXY climbs back above 100 and is closing in on a new 4-month high
data.description:
The U.S. dollar strengthened further on Friday, with the U.S. Dollar Index (DXY) climbing roughly 0.5% and reclaiming the key psychological threshold of 100.

The index now trades near 100.21, marking its highest level in nearly four months and reflecting renewed momentum in the greenback. 

Friday’s advance places the dollar at its strongest point since November 21, underscoring a steady rebound from its year-to-date low of 95.55 recorded on back on January 27. Since that trough, the index has gained approximately 4.8%, highlighting a sustained shift in currency markets toward a firmer U.S. dollar. 

Technical indicators also point to strengthening momentum. The DXY is now trading comfortably above its 50-day, 100-day, and 200-day moving averages, levels closely watched by market participants to gauge longer-term trend direction. 

The move higher signals growing demand for the dollar amid evolving macroeconomic conditions and global market developments. As the currency continues to hold above key technical thresholds, traders will be watching whether the index can maintain its position above the 100 level and extend the rally in the sessions ahead. 

U.S. Dollar ETFs: (UUP), (USDU), and (UDN). 

Treasury ETFs: (TLT), (TLH), (IEF), (IEI), (SHY), (SGOV), (SCHO), and (BIL).

S&P 500 ETFs: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), (VFFSX), and (SWPPX).

data.title: Wall Street slides as investors assess PCE inflation and GDP data
data.description:
Wall Street’s major indexes moved lower on Friday as investors digested a fresh batch of economic data, highlighted by the latest PCE inflation and GDP reports.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.2%, the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was near even, and the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.4%. 

From a sector-by-sector stance, five of the 11 S&P sectors were in the green with utilities being the strongest performing area on the session while industrials have been the weakest. 

On the economic front, core PCE inflation rose 3.1% Y/Y in January, a touch hotter than expected. Furthermore, the U.S. GDP growth estimate was revised lower to +0.7% in Q4. 

Additionally, durable goods orders growth has stalled in the month of January. The January job openings increased more than expected in the latest JOLTS report and consumer sentiment data declined more than expected in March

U.S. Treasury yields were muted. The 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) stood at 3.72%, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was at 4.26%, and the 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) was at 4.89%. 

As for stocks that are on the move, Darden Restaurants (DRI) climbed up +2%, and shares of Adobe (ADBE) fell -6.4%.

data.title: A victim of its success? Ulta fails to clear Wall Street's high bar
data.description:
With the stock down another 9% on Friday after a steep sell-off in Thursday’s after-hours trading, the price action of Ulta (ULTA) compared to its fourth-quarter results looks like the stock might be the victim of Wall Street’s overly ambitious expectations.

Fourth-quarter results were broadly positive, with strong comparable store sales supported by share gains across most categories. Still, a few pockets of softness unsettled investors.

Higher SG&A costs during the quarter weighed on margins and shaved roughly $1 off EPS, both of which fell short of expectations.

Wall Street analysts estimate that half of these costs were driven by incentive compensation and higher marketing costs that fueled Q4 sales outperformance. However, in FY26, SG&A is expected to remain inflated from prior levels during the first half of the year “with a better setup in H2 as heavy H2 2025 investments roll off,” says Jefferies’ Sydney Wagner.

Guidance was weighed on sentiment even though the company is projecting healthy sales growth of 6% to 7%, comparable sales growth of 2.5% to 3.5%, and profit growth of 10% from FY25 at the midpoint of the $28.05 and $28.55 range.

These projections look “conservative” to J.P. Morgan’s Christopher Horvers and views the estimated 150 basis point deceleration in assumed industry growth that baselines Ulta’s same-store sales outlook as “prudent.” 

“The reasonable view is that ULTA is being conservative, and Q4 was never the quarter to assess expense leverage potential. We estimate that ULTA will get back roughly half of the estimated total $60M of incentive comp headwinds from 2025,” he writes, recommending investors buy the pullback in the stock with his updated $29.10 2026 EPS estimate above management’s outlook.

Oppenheimer’s Rupesh Parikh also sees the pullback as a buying opportunity.

“Ulta shares were priced to perfection, in a high expectation setup, so we believe the initial re-rating lower makes sense. We would take advantage of the dip but expect a volatile trade to continue amidst geopolitical uncertainty and increasing concerns on discretionary spending.”

So far, investors are not taking the bait as Ulta (ULTA) shares gapped lower at Friday’s open but, at the same time, have respected support at the 200-day moving average, a level that has not been breached in nearly a year.
